findings.description: The HTML introduces fabricated table headers that do not appear in the PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML adds a table with header row: "Time" and "Activity". In the PDF, the agenda is presented as a timed list without any visible table header labels.

findings.description: The HTML injects internal conversion comments and structural text not present in the PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML contains the comment text "<!-- COLUMNS: Time | Activity -->", which is not part of the source PDF content.

findings.description: The PDF does not contain a bordered data table, but the HTML converts the agenda into a formal two-column table with added headers, altering the original structure.
findings.evidence: PDF shows agenda items as aligned text entries by time; HTML wraps nearly the full agenda in a <table> with columns "Time" and "Activity".

findings.description: Heading hierarchy in the HTML does not accurately reflect the visual title structure of the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF presents "Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting" and "AGENDA" as part of the main title block, with "Thursday, May 14" as a section heading. HTML uses <h1> for "Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting", <h2> for "AGENDA", and <h3> for "Thursday, May 14", which is a reasonable conversion but does not clearly preserve the co-equal emphasis of the title block.

findings.description: Header/footer content is duplicated within the middle of the HTML body due to page-break handling.
findings.evidence: The page header "Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda" appears at the top and again inside the 10:00 a.m. table cell; the footer note "* Times indicated are merely for guidance. The commission will proceed through the agenda until completed. **Probable Action Items" is also inserted mid-table and then repeated at the end. In the PDF these are page header/footer artifacts, not body content.

findings.description: Some punctuation/spacing differs from the PDF in ways that may affect exact fidelity.
findings.evidence: PDF: "Blue Crab Fishery Management Plan Amendment 3 –Corrin Flora, Joe Facendola" and "North Carolina– Shannon Jenkins and Shawn Nelson**" appear with specific spacing/dash rendering. HTML normalizes some of these inconsistently and also appends footer page numbers into the same text block, e.g., "**Probable Action Items 1" and "**Probable Action Items 2".
